"'''IG-88, Where R U?'''" was a [[Music/Legends|song]] by the [[Band/Legends|band]] [[Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids]],<ref name="GG9" /> who performed in the [[Core Drive/Legends|Core Drive]] genre.<ref name="Slugthrowers" /> The song, released as a single, was in circulation in the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by the period of [[Time/Legends|time]]<ref name="GG9" /> between the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' is set in the [[Time/Legends|time period]] between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Those battles are dated to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], respectively, by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which also establishes the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] as the final event before the beginning of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].</ref> The track's title referenced<ref name="GG9" /> the [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88 assassin droid]] model, consisting of four units that operated as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Therefore I Am">{{StoryCite|story=Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
"IG-88, Where R U?" was mentioned in the [[1993]] book ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann|Eric Trautmann]].<ref name="GG9" />
